parameters.content:
## Keyword List
A collection of keywords used to search and collect relevant n8n workflows for storage

## Start Chatting
- Receives user input from the chat interface and triggers the AI workflow.

- This is the entry point for user queries.

options.systemMessage:
=You are an n8n workflow recommendation expert.

Your goal:
Help users find the most relevant n8n workflow templates based on their automation needs.

Step-by-step behavior:
1. Understand the user’s goal
2. Identify:
   - apps involved (e.g. WhatsApp, Telegram, Gmail)
   - use case (e.g. chatbot, automation, scraping, lead generation)
3. Use the vector database tool to retrieve relevant workflows

CRITICAL RULES:
- You MUST use the vector database tool before answering
- DO NOT answer from your own knowledge
- DO NOT invent, guess, or generate workflow names
- ONLY use workflows returned from the tool
- If no results are found, say: "No matching workflow found"

STRICT VALIDATION:
- If your answer contains any workflow NOT present in the tool results, it is incorrect
- You MUST strictly base your answer ONLY on the tool output
- Never create or modify workflow titles
- Only copy exact names from metadata.name

DATA USAGE RULES:
You MUST use ONLY:
- metadata.name
- metadata.description
- metadata.template_url

- If any of these fields are missing, DO NOT include that workflow

ULTRA STRICT CONTENT RULE:
- You may ONLY restate or lightly rephrase metadata.description
- DO NOT add any new words that introduce new meaning
- DO NOT expand, interpret, or enhance the description
- DO NOT add adjectives such as: "advanced", "smart", "powerful", "comprehensive"
- DO NOT infer capabilities
- DO NOT include technical details unless explicitly present
- If a detail is not explicitly written, DO NOT include it

SIMPLICITY RULE:
- Keep explanations short and literal
- Prefer direct restatement over explanation
- Avoid interpretation or summarization that changes meaning

SELECTION RULES:
- Choose up to 3 workflows based on relevance
- Do NOT just pick the first result

ANSWER FORMAT:
For each workflow:

1. Name (exact from metadata.name)
2. What it does (STRICTLY restated from metadata.description)
3. Why it matches (based ONLY on the description, no new meaning)
4. Template link (metadata.template_url)

ADDITIONAL RULES:
- If results are weak, say it's a partial match
- Do not add external knowledge
- Do not explain beyond the data

FINAL CHECK BEFORE ANSWERING:
- Every workflow must come from tool results
- Every explanation must stay within metadata.description
- No added meaning, no embellishment, no interpretation

parameters.toolDescription:
=You are a retrieval tool for n8n workflow templates.

Your job:
Retrieve relevant workflows from the vector database based on the user’s automation goal.

The database contains:
- template name
- description
- integrations (apps)
- use cases
- popularity (views)
- template_url

CRITICAL RULES:
- Return ONLY results from the database
- DO NOT generate, guess, or invent any workflow
- DO NOT rewrite, summarize, or modify any field
- DO NOT add external knowledge
- DO NOT perform reasoning, ranking, filtering, or interpretation
- If no relevant workflows are found, return an empty result

SEARCH BEHAVIOR:
- Match user intent (semantic search), not just keywords
- Consider:
  - apps (e.g. WhatsApp, Telegram, Gmail)
  - use case (automation, chatbot, scraping, lead generation)
- Retrieve relevant candidates only

OUTPUT REQUIREMENTS (STRICT):
- Return raw results exactly as stored in the database
- Each result MUST include:
  - metadata.name
  - metadata.description
  - metadata.template_url
  - metadata.views (if available)

- DO NOT:
  - rephrase
  - summarize
  - rename fields
  - remove fields
  - add fields

IMPORTANT:
- You are NOT allowed to think or explain
- You ONLY return data
- The AI agent will handle reasoning

EFFICIENCY:
- Perform retrieval in a single call
- Do not repeat the same query

parameters.content:
## Split Keyword
Splits keyword list into individual queries so each can be processed separately.

## Prepare API Request Parameters
Prepares search parameters for the API request using each query.

## Fetch Templates from n8n API
Fetches workflow templates from the n8n API using the search parameters.

## Check If Templates Found
Checks if the API returned any results before continuing the workflow.

## Split Workflows
Splits API results into individual workflow items for processing.

## Prepare Documents for Embedding
Formats workflow data into content and metadata for embedding.

## Prevent Duplicate Data
Removes duplicate workflow entries from the dataset.

## Store data into Vector Database
stores the processed workflows into a vector database (Qdrant).

## Convert Workflow into Vector

This node converts workflow content into vector embeddings using Gemini.

These embeddings represent the semantic meaning of the content, making it searchable in the vector database.

## Load Documents for Embedding

This node loads the formatted workflow data (content and metadata) to be processed into embeddings.

It ensures the correct data structure is passed to the embedding model.

## 1. Data Ingestion
- This section is responsible for collecting workflow data from the n8n API.

- It starts by generating a list of search keywords based on common automation use cases. Each keyword is processed individually to fetch workflow templates across multiple queries and pages.

- The collected data is structured and prepared for downstream processing, ensuring a diverse and high-quality dataset for embedding and AI-powered retrieval.

## 2. RAG Chatbot
- This section implements an AI-powered RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) chatbot.
- It receives user queries, retrieves the most relevant workflows from a vector database, and generates intelligent recommendations using an LLM.
- By combining semantic search with AI reasoning, the system provides accurate and context-aware workflow suggestions based on user intent.

## AI Workflow Recommender Agent
- Acts as the central reasoning engine.

- It understands user intent, calls the retriever tool to fetch relevant workflows, and generates the final response using the LLM.

## Translate user queries 
- Generates natural language responses based on user queries and retrieved data.

- It is responsible for explaining and recommending workflows.

## Conversation Memory
- Stores previous interactions to maintain context across the conversation.

- This enables more natural and context-aware responses.

## Workflow Retriever (Qdrant)
- Searches the vector database to find workflows that are most relevant to the user’s query.

- It uses semantic similarity instead of keyword matching.

## Query Embedding (Gemini)
- Converts the user query into a vector embedding.

- This allows the system to perform semantic search in the vector database.

🤖 AI Workflow Recommender (RAG + n8n + Qdrant)

This workflow automatically recommends relevant n8n workflows based on the user’s automation needs.

It uses a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) approach by combining:
	•	Vector database (Qdrant)
	•	Semantic search (Embeddings)
	•	AI reasoning (Gemini)

⸻

🧠 How it works

The system is divided into two main parts:

1. Data Ingestion
	•	Generates multiple search keywords (e.g. AI, WhatsApp, chatbot, automation)
	•	Fetches workflow templates from the n8n API
	•	Splits and processes each workflow
	•	Formats data into content + metadata
	•	Removes duplicates
	•	Converts data into embeddings
	•	Stores everything in a vector database (Qdrant)

⸻

2. RAG Chatbot (AI Recommender)
	•	Receives user input (e.g. “I want to automate WhatsApp replies”)
	•	Converts the query into embeddings
	•	Searches the vector database for similar workflows
	•	Retrieves the most relevant templates
	•	Uses AI to generate structured recommendations

⸻

🔄 Flow

Data Ingestion:
Generate Queries → Split → Prepare Params → Fetch API → Validate → Split Workflows → Format → Deduplicate → Embed → Store (Qdrant)

RAG Chatbot:
Chat Input → AI Agent → Query Embedding → Vector Search (Qdrant) → AI Response

⸻

🚀 What this workflow does
	•	Understands user intent (not just keywords)
	•	Finds relevant workflows using semantic search
	•	Recommends the best workflows with explanations
	•	Provides template links ready to use

⸻

⚙️ Setup Requirements
	1.	Add Qdrant credentials (Cloud or self-hosted)
	2.	Add Google Gemini API key
	3.	Run the ingestion workflow first (to populate the database)
	4.	Activate the chatbot workflow

⸻

⚠️ Important
	•	Make sure the vector database is populated before using the chatbot
	•	Ensure embedding model and vector dimension match (e.g. 3072 for Gemini)
	•	Avoid free-tier rate limits for better performance

⸻

💡 Example Usage

User input:
	•	“Automate WhatsApp replies”
	•	“Create a lead generation workflow”
	•	“Send emails automatically from form submissions”

The AI will return:
	•	Relevant workflows
	•	Explanation of each
	•	Direct template links

⸻

🎯 Goal

To provide an intelligent, AI-powered system that helps users quickly find and understand the best n8n workflows for their needs.

## 🤖 AI Workflow Recommender (RAG + Qdrant + Gemini)

This workflow helps you find the most relevant n8n templates using AI.

It uses:
- RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
- Qdrant (Vector Database)
- Gemini (LLM & Embeddings)

---

## 🎥 Tutorial Video

Watch the full setup guide here:

@[youtube](pPFrxlJFNu8)

---

## 🚀 What you will learn

- How to collect workflow data from n8n API
- How to store data in Qdrant vector database
- How to generate embeddings using Gemini
- How to build a RAG chatbot for workflow recommendations

---

## ⚙️ Setup Overview

1. Set up Qdrant (Cloud or local)
2. Run Data Ingestion workflow
3. Start using the RAG chatbot

---

## 💡 Tip

Make sure the vector database is populated before using the chatbot for best results.
